Howard Marks (Oaktree) Memo — "The Pendulum Swings Again" (March 2026)

The market is once again in a state where enthusiasm outweighs caution. AI-related stocks have driven the S&P 500 to new highs, and the Nasdaq has exceeded its 2024 peak by 40%. History doesn't repeat, but it rhymes.

Key Observations:

1. Second-Level Thinking on AI: "Everyone knows AI will be transformational. The question isn't whether AI is important — it's whether AI stocks are priced appropriately given the uncertainty about who will capture the value. When consensus is overwhelmingly bullish, the risk-reward is usually poor."

2. Credit Spreads: Investment-grade spreads have compressed to 90 bps over Treasuries, near historical lows. High-yield spreads at 280 bps suggest minimal risk premium. "When spreads are this tight, you're being paid to ignore risk, not to take it."

3. AI CapEx Sustainability: "Microsoft, Google, Amazon, and Meta are spending $200B+ annually on AI infrastructure. This is the biggest capital expenditure cycle since the telecom buildout of the late 1990s. Not all of this spending will generate adequate returns."

4. Interest Rate Cycle: "The Fed has cut rates to 3.25%, but the era of zero rates is unlikely to return. A higher neutral rate (2.5-3%) means the margin of safety for leveraged investments is thinner."

5. Concentration Risk: "The top 10 stocks now represent 38% of the S&P 500 market cap. This level of concentration has historically preceded periods of mean reversion."

6. Quality Over Growth: "In the current environment, I favor companies with pricing power, low leverage, and predictable free cash flow over companies with exciting narratives but uncertain unit economics."

Investment Implications:
Oaktree is positioning defensively: reducing exposure to public equities, accumulating dry powder in distressed credit, and emphasizing real assets. "We're not predicting a crash. We're positioning for the possibility that returns from this point will be lower than returns to this point."

Data Date: 2026-03-05
